identified_failure_cases.question: Big-O notation and O(n)/O(n log n)/O(n²)
identified_failure_cases.root_cause: Big-O notation uses mathematical symbols (O(n log n), asymptotic) that resist natural-language KG concept extraction. Most answers show n_matched=1 even for high-quality answers. Impoverished KG evidence leads verifier to undergrade.

specific_cases.answer: I don't know what Big-O means.
It measures how fast code runs.
O(n²) is always bad. O(n) is always good.
Big-O notation tells us how efficient code is.
Big-O is how we measure algorithm speed. Smaller Big-O is better.
Big-O measures how long an algorithm takes. O(n) is linear, O(n²) is slower.
Big-O describes algorithm complexity. O(n) means linear, O(n²) means quadratic.
Big-O measures how fast an algorithm's time grows with input size. O(n) is linea
Big-O gives the growth rate of an algorithm's time. O(n) is linear, O(n log n) a
Big-O describes worst-case growth rate as n increases. O(n) is linear, O(n log n
Big-O is the asymptotic upper bound on time growth. O(n) grows linearly — each a

identified_failure_cases.question: Binary Search Tree operations
identified_failure_cases.root_cause: Minor: one outlier (ID 42) where verifier over-penalized vague answer.

general_limitation: ConceptGrade's benefit depends on KG vocabulary coverage. Topics with formal mathematical notation or specialized symbols that resist natural-language concept extraction show reduced gains. The KG matching layer should be extended to include mathematical relationship nodes for notation-heavy topics.
